step_by_step_thinking:
VLDL, LDL, HDL, and chylomicrons are different types of lipoproteins. Among these, only one does not migrate toward the cathode during electrophoretic separation due to its size and charge characteristics. Given that VLDL, LDL, and HDL do contain significant amounts of protein and thus carry some electrical charge, whereas chylomicrons primarily consist of triacylglycerols and phospholipids with minimal proteins, chylomicrons will remain near the origin rather than migrating significantly.
\boxed{D} Based on the information about the composition and behavior of various lipoproteins during electrophoresis, chylomicrons stand out as predominantly composed of triacylglycerols and phospholipids with minimal protein content compared to VLDL, LDL, and HDL. Therefore, chylomicrons do not migrate towards the negatively charged electrode during electrophoresis.
